mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is directional only when the policy rate is declining from a prior month and not when it is flat or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration raises expected policy restraint and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when inflation momentum breaches a high threshold.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month median by 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation-momentum months are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect flips positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and rising recession odds, which depress expected earnings and equity risk appetite. The mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when unemployment is rising on a three-month basis.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of unemployment-rise episodes, or if the signal fails to beat unconditional direction accuracy by at least 5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE is rising, the labor market is contracting in a way that directly threatens revenue and margins. This joint deterioration should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because earnings expectations are revised down more than rates can support valuations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE does not produce negative 60d SPX returns in at least 60% of observations, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and better near-term revenue momentum for S&P 500 firms, supporting positive returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when sales growth improves from a prior month rather than merely staying elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month RSAFS increases are not followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL growth direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and better capacity utilization, which supports cyclical earnings and risk appetite. This should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when production is expanding for at least two consecutive months.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO expansion periods do not precede positive 60d SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling 3-year directional accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad final-demand acceleration, which should lift equity earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when GDP improves but retail sales do not confirm the demand impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60d SPX returns, or if confirmed-demand episodes fail to show positive returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp HOUST contraction signals housing-led demand weakness and tighter cyclical credit conditions, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is most negative after HOUST falls below a recent three-month average and remains weak for a second month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 3-month moving average for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if repeated HOUST weakness is not followed by negative 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered observations, or if the effect is null once UNRATE is stable.
